skippedSources.name: Seven C Newsletter #173
skippedSources.reason: Link-dump format with no substantive analysis

items.title: Starmer Expected to Announce Exit Monday Morning — Burnham Succession Begins
items.idea: Andy Burnham's Makerfield by-election win (54.8% vote share, 24,927 votes; Reform UK at 34.5%) gave him the parliamentary seat needed to challenge for Labour leader. Multiple outlets (Guardian, Bloomberg, BBC) expect Starmer to set an exit timetable Monday morning. The hard bargaining is now around the Treasury appointment — Burnham's camp is already debating fiscal appointments while UK borrowing hit £23.3bn in May (overshooting), and bond investors will price the chancellor before they price the slogans. A July 22 UK-EU summit gives Brussels a reason to wait for the next mandate rather than bank concessions from a lame duck.
items.angle: Markets haven't fully priced a chancellor change under a Burnham government. UK gilts and sterling are the trades. The July 22 EU summit is now a 'wait and see' event for Brussels.
items.tweetA: Britain is getting a new PM without a general election. The market is focused on who leads. Wrong question. The real question is: who becomes chancellor? Burnham's camp is already debating fiscal appointments while UK May borrowing hit £23.3bn. Bond investors price the team before they price the speech.
items.tweetB: Burnham won Makerfield 54.8% vs Reform's 34.5%. A mayor became an MP — the constitutional threshold. Now watch the July 22 UK-EU summit: Brussels has every incentive to wait for the next mandate rather than bank concessions from a PM who may be packing boxes.

items.source: Princeton Chen / Tiger Capital + P Equity Research + Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: Iran Talks Enter 'Difficult Phase' — Trump Threats Derail Switzerland Summit
items.idea: The first-ever high-level US-Iran meeting occurred at the Lake Lucerne Summit (Vance + Witkoff + Kushner vs. Qalibaf + Araghchi). But Trump simultaneously threatened from Camp David to 'hit Iran VERY HARD again, only harder.' Iran left the table wanting a Trump apology before returning. Brent +2%+ Monday morning. Pentagon says full Hormuz mine clearance takes up to 6 months; insurers cite 40-50 days before carriers transit. Kalshi gives 50/50 odds traffic normalizes by Sept 1. Graham publicly said if talks fail, 'Trump takes the Strait by force and charges a fee.'
items.angle: Oil risk premium is NOT priced in. Mine clearance is 6 months. Insurance gap is 40-50 days. The fat left tail on oil supply disruption is massively underpriced if talks collapse.
items.tweetA: Everyone's celebrating the Lake Lucerne handshake. Here's what the market isn't pricing: mine clearance takes up to 6 months. Insurers need 40-50 days before carriers transit. And Trump simultaneously threatened to 'hit Iran VERY HARD again, only harder.' The peace premium is not ready for collection.
items.tweetB: Iran left Switzerland after Trump's threats. Wants an apology before returning. Kalshi: 50/50 Hormuz normalizes by Sept 1. Graham: 'If talks fail, Trump takes the Strait by force and charges a fee.' The distribution of outcomes here is extremely fat-tailed. $BNO $USO

items.title: A Trillion Dollars in the Ground: AI Capex Curve Is Identical to 1996-2001 Telecom
items.idea: The four largest hyperscalers spent $416B on capex in 2025 and have guided $725B in 2026 (+77% in one year). Amazon's TTM FCF collapsed 95%, from $38.2B to $1.2B. Aggregate capex-to-depreciation ratio is 3.9x (Oracle at 8x, Meta 3.8x, Microsoft 3.5x). Index the telecom capex curve against the AI hyperscaler curve: the shapes are nearly identical for the first four years. The telecom curve broke in year five. Less than 5% of the fiber buried 1996-2001 was ever lit; most builders went bankrupt.
items.angle: Second-wave buyers after first-wave bankruptcy are the real AI beneficiaries. Not the hyperscalers. Companies that will acquire data center assets at distressed prices. We're in 1999.
items.tweetA: Less than 5% of the fiber buried during the 1996-2001 telecom buildout was ever lit. The builders went bankrupt. The shapes of that capex curve and the current AI hyperscaler curve are nearly identical — four years in. The telecom curve broke in year five. We're in 1999.
items.tweetB: Amazon FCF just collapsed 95% — from $38.2B to $1.2B — while hyperscalers guide $725B in capex for 2026. Capex-to-depreciation is 3.9x across the group. The technology is real. That's exactly what they said about the fiber.

items.title: AI Debt Is Now 14% of the JP Morgan US IG Index — Bigger Than US Banks
items.idea: AI-tied corporate bond issuance hit $121B in 2025, a 332% surge vs. the 2020-2024 baseline of ~$28B/year. Meta priced $30B in September 2025; Oracle priced $18B in October plus arranged $38B in project finance. AI debt now represents ~14% of the JP Morgan US investment-grade index — larger weight than US banks.
items.angle: The IG credit market is now an AI infrastructure market. Any crack in AI revenue expectations doesn't just hit equities — it reprices $121B+ of bonds. Credit is pricing zero probability of a telecom-style bust.
items.tweetA: AI debt is now 14% of the JP Morgan US investment-grade index. Larger than US banks. In one year: $121 billion issued, up 332% from the prior baseline. The equity story is well-known. The credit story is just beginning. Watch IG spreads when hyperscaler FCF misses start compounding.
items.tweetB: Meta borrowed $30B in September. Oracle borrowed $18B in October plus $38B in project finance. The IG market is now an AI infrastructure market — financing buildouts on the assumption that demand catches supply before the cash runs out. Pricing zero default risk.

items.title: CoreWeave Is the WorldCom of This AI Cycle
items.idea: CoreWeave carries ~$14.8B in debt against D/E ratio of ~4.5x, generated -$7.25B in FCF last year, and derives ~70% of its revenue from OpenAI (itself unprofitable). Structurally WorldCom: pure-play infrastructure operator, levered, single-customer concentration, financing ahead of demand. 'Not fraud — but in the structural sense. That story has a known ending.'
items.angle: CoreWeave Nasdaq-100 inclusion today creates forced buying right when fundamental thesis is most fragile. Event-driven short after index flows clear?
items.tweetA: CoreWeave: $14.8B in debt, 4.5x D/E, -$7.25B FCF, 70% revenue from OpenAI. OpenAI is not profitable. CoreWeave is not profitable. This is structural WorldCom. Not fraud — the same structural story: pure-play infra, levered, single-customer, building ahead of demand. That story has a known ending.
items.tweetB: Today CoreWeave gets added to the Nasdaq-100. Index funds are forced buyers. Meanwhile: $14.8B debt, -$7.25B FCF, 70% revenue concentration in one unprofitable customer. Index inclusion is not a thesis. It's a timing mechanism. Stay alert to what happens after forced flows clear.

items.title: 95% of Organizations Report Zero ROI on Generative AI (MIT Project NANDA)
items.idea: Microsoft annualized AI revenue ~$37B against $97B LTM capex (~38% coverage). MIT Project NANDA: 95% of organizations report zero return on generative AI. RAND: 80.3% of AI projects fail. Gartner: 60% of enterprise AI projects cancelled by end-2026. McKinsey: 6% of companies see >5% EBIT contribution. Pivotal Research: Alphabet FCF declines ~90% between 2025 and 2026 if it executes $180-185B capex guidance.
items.angle: The enterprise adoption story is the load-bearing wall of the AI capex thesis. It may be cracking. Watch Q2 earnings calls for AI cost disclosure without revenue offset.
items.tweetA: 95% of organizations report zero return on generative AI (MIT Project NANDA). 80% of AI projects fail (RAND). 60% cancelled by 2026 (Gartner). 6% see material earnings contribution (McKinsey). Microsoft has $37B in AI revenue against $97B in capex. The demand side is not showing up.
items.tweetB: Pivotal Research: Alphabet FCF could fall 90% between 2025 and 2026 if it executes $180-185B capex. That's what a company does when it's financing infrastructure it hopes to monetize — eventually. The 15-year habit of rising cash returns is about to change.

items.title: Power Grid Can't Handle AI: ERCOT Demand 4.3x by 2032, Data Centers 12% of US Power by 2028
items.idea: PJM 2026/27 capacity auction cleared at $329.17/MW-day — the FERC-approved cap, up 22% from $269.92 the prior year. ERCOT projects peak demand of 367,790 MW by 2032 against 85,508 MW in 2023 — 4.3x increase driven primarily by data centers. DOE: data centers will consume 12% of US power by 2028, up from 4.4% in 2023. The grid is not configured to deliver that power on the AI buildout timeline.
items.angle: Grid constraint = floor on energy infrastructure investment. Nuclear, natural gas peakers, transmission equipment. Multi-year structural tailwind for power infrastructure companies.
items.tweetA: ERCOT is projecting 4.3x peak demand growth by 2032. 367,790 MW vs 85,508 MW today. Almost entirely data centers. DOE: data centers consume 12% of US power by 2028, up from 4.4% today. The AI capex cycle has a physics problem. The grid is not being built fast enough to power it.
items.tweetB: PJM cleared its capacity auction at the FERC cap: $329/MW-day, up 22% year-over-year. When a power auction clears at the maximum allowed price, supply is failing to keep up. AI compute needs power. Power infrastructure can't scale as fast as software can promise. That gap is a structural trade.

items.title: Inference Cost Down 87% in 12 Months — The Bull Case That Could Change Everything
items.idea: Mary Meeker's 2025 AI Trends report: inference cost fell ~87% over twelve months. If deflation continues, the gap between AI capex and AI revenue closes from the demand side — cheaper inference unlocks workloads currently uneconomic. Stanley Druckenmiller opened new positions in Amazon, Meta, and Alphabet in Q3 2025, betting the market overcorrected on near-term FCF and underpriced AI revenue maturation optionality.
items.angle: 87% cost decline in one year is extraordinary. Bull case is a race condition: rate of inference cost decline vs. rate of debt maturation. Druckenmiller's bet is that cost deflation wins the race.
items.tweetA: The bear case on AI capex is compelling. The one thing that breaks it: inference cost dropped 87% in 12 months (Mary Meeker). If that curve holds two more years, today's uneconomic workloads all pencil out. Druckenmiller bought Amazon, Meta, Alphabet in Q3 2025 after the FCF concerns were public. That's the counter.
items.tweetB: 87% inference cost deflation in 12 months. If that continues, the 95% enterprise zero-ROI number from NANDA becomes stale in 18 months as new use cases open up. The bull case: cost deflation creates the market that justifies the capex retroactively. The race condition.

items.title: Micron Earnings Wednesday: DRAM Prices +700% YoY, Estimates Look Too Low
items.idea: Micron reports Wednesday after the close. Street estimates: $20 EPS, $35B revenue. DRAM contract prices surged 700% YoY with more increases expected. Analyst predicts $27.90 EPS (37% beat) and $42.3B revenue (21% beat). Last quarter Micron beat guidance by 48% ($33.5B vs $22.5B estimate). At 'trillionaire status,' a negative reaction could trigger Nasdaq/semiconductor selloff. Last earnings: fell 3.8% post-report then corrected 30%.
items.angle: Reaction matters more than the beat. At $1T market cap, even a 37% earnings beat can be met with 'sell the news.' Key: guidance spread. 48% last quarter. Margin trajectory equally critical. This is a vol event.
items.tweetA: DRAM prices up 700% year-over-year. Micron reports Wednesday. Street expects $20 EPS / $35B revenue. Last quarter they beat the estimate by 48%. The beat is coming. The question is whether $1T market cap can absorb it or if 'sell the news' wins. Watch the guidance spread, not the earnings headline. $MU
items.tweetB: Micron fell 3.8% after its last earnings beat — then corrected 30% over the following weeks. At trillionaire status, near-perfection gets punished. Setup: DRAM +700% YoY, massive beat incoming, but the post-earnings behavior defines the semiconductor sector's Q3 trajectory. This is a vol event. $MU

items.title: Nasdaq-100 Rebalance Today: CoreWeave, Nebius, Astera Labs, Rocket Lab, Teradyne Added
items.idea: Nasdaq-100 quarterly rebalance June 22 (today). Stocks added: Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius, Rocket Lab, Teradyne. Mandatory institutional buying flows from all NDX-tracking ETFs and funds. CoreWeave's inclusion ($14.8B debt, -$7.25B FCF) creates a forced bid disconnected from fundamentals. Astera Labs (AI photonic networking) and Nebius (AI cloud infrastructure) are the cleaner AI-infra expressions.
items.angle: Nasdaq-100 additions see buying pressure on rebalance day, then often fade. Names with weak fundamentals (CoreWeave) are post-flow short candidates. ALAB and NBIS have stronger fundamental stories.
items.tweetA: Nasdaq-100 rebalances today. Added: CoreWeave, Nebius, Astera Labs, Rocket Lab, Teradyne. Index funds are forced buyers right now. Mechanical flow, not a thesis. The two worth watching after the flow clears: $ALAB and $NBIS — AI infrastructure with actual fundamentals.
items.tweetB: CoreWeave gets added to the Nasdaq-100 today. -$7.25B FCF, 70% revenue from OpenAI, $14.8B in debt. The index doesn't care. Forced buying happens regardless. This is the AI cycle in one event: passive flows create demand for assets whose fundamentals haven't caught up to their story.

items.title: CLARITY Act on Senate Floor Now — 7 Democrats Away From Crypto Law
items.idea: The CLARITY Act is physically on the Senate floor. Remaining math: 7 Democratic votes. White House July 4 signing target stands. Standard Chartered estimates XRP ETFs could pull $8B in flows if bill passes. The bill codifies crypto as commodity status — a structural change to US regulatory framework. XRP at $1.14 with $1.10 as must-hold support is the highest-conviction event play into early July. A major Wall Street bank filed amended spot-ETF applications for both Ethereum and Solana.
items.angle: The 7-Democrat calculation is the catalyst that can flip the entire alt market. If CLARITY passes before July 4, the $8B Standard Chartered estimate looks conservative. Most traders focused on BTC dollar headwind — the regulatory catalyst is more powerful.
items.tweetA: CLARITY Act is on the Senate floor. 7 Democrats. That's the only math left. White House July 4 target still stands. Standard Chartered says XRP ETFs pull $8B if it passes. That number looks conservative if institutional money waiting for regulatory clarity actually shows up. $XRP
items.tweetB: The crypto market is focused on BTC at $64K and whether the dollar breaks out. Meanwhile CLARITY is one procedural vote away from the floor. The bill that codifies crypto as a commodity. If 7 Democrats move before July 4, the entire regulatory thesis reprices. $XRP $1.10 is the stop.

items.title: Franklin Templeton Files Bitcoin DRIP ETFs — Equity Dividends Reinvested Into BTC
items.idea: Franklin Templeton filed for two novel 'Bitcoin DRIP' ETFs: hold US equities, reinvest dividends into Bitcoin. Effective as early as September 1. Creates structural demand channel routing equity dividend flows into BTC automatically — no crypto-native investor required. Separately: major Wall Street bank filed amended spot-ETF applications for both ETH AND SOL. Bitmine holds 5.62M ETH. Strategy holds 846,842 BTC at ~$75,700 cost basis.
items.angle: Equity dividend flows are enormous and largely automatic. Even a small percentage routed into BTC via this product creates a persistent mechanical bid from equity income investors who never thought of themselves as crypto buyers.
items.tweetA: Franklin Templeton just filed for Bitcoin DRIP ETFs. Hold US stocks. Reinvest dividends into BTC. Effective September 1. No crypto native required. Your equity dividend becomes a BTC accumulation vehicle. Structural bid, not a trade.
items.tweetB: The Bitcoin ETF evolution: first direct BTC ETFs, now equity funds redirecting dividend flows into BTC. Franklin Templeton's DRIP ETFs route stock dividends into Bitcoin automatically. Different investor pool — equity income investors — being given a passive BTC accumulation mechanism.

items.source: Princeton Chen / Tiger Capital + Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: Dollar Index on the Verge of a Major Breakout — The Dominant Crypto Headwind
items.idea: DXY 'on the verge of a major breakout,' described as crypto's most direct headwind. 'Warsh killed crypto's rate-cut trade' — new Fed Chair Warsh presiding over hawkish FOMC, one 2026 hike fully priced, 2026 median dot at 3.8%, PCE forecast raised to 3.6%. BTC and gold the only two major assets red in 2026 (BTC -26.5% YTD, SOL -45% YTD). Crypto trades dollar liquidity and Fed policy, not oil. Japan's yen near a 40-year low, creating intervention risk.
items.angle: The DXY breakout is the sleeper risk for all risk assets. The rate-cut trade that powered 2024-2025 returns is gone until September at earliest. Short JPY intervention risk could create brief dollar weakness — a vol event, not a trend reversal.
items.tweetA: Warsh's first FOMC: one 2026 hike fully priced, median dot at 3.8%, PCE forecast raised to 3.6%. The dollar is on the verge of a major breakout. BTC and gold — the only major assets red in 2026. The rate-cut trade is over. Crypto trades the dollar, not the peace deal.
items.tweetB: Japan's yen near a 40-year low. Intervention bets building. When the BoJ moves, yen strengthens fast — a global liquidity event. In 2022 and 2024, yen strength correlated with risk-off across all assets. The dollar/yen dynamic is the macro wildcard getting underweighted against Iran noise.

items.title: PCE & GDP Thursday: April at 3.8%, 9/18 Fed Dots Already See a 2026 Hike
items.idea: Thursday brings May PCE (Fed's preferred inflation metric), Q1 GDP final print (1.6%, revised down from 2.0%), plus durable goods and jobless claims. April PCE was +3.8%. With 9 of 18 FOMC dots projecting a 2026 hike and median dot at 3.8%, a PCE surprise higher could push one-hike pricing to two. GDP propped up by hyperscaler capex — vulnerable if AI investment decelerates. Treasury auctions 2/5/7-year notes Tues-Thurs.
items.angle: PCE Thursday is the most important data point of the week — determines whether September dot plot softens. The entire 2H risk rally thesis depends on this print cooling.
items.tweetA: PCE Thursday. April was 3.8%. 9 of 18 FOMC members already project a 2026 rate hike. Median dot at 3.8%. If May PCE surprises higher, the one-hike trade becomes two-hike. Q1 GDP was revised to 1.6% on weaker consumer and inventory drawdowns. The economy is being carried by hyperscaler capex. What happens when they blink?
items.tweetB: This week's Fed speakers will tell you nothing new. The data will. May PCE on Thursday is the 60-90 day path to a less-hawkish September dot plot. A miss to the upside locks in a hike. A cool print opens the rate-cut trade that's been priced out all year. The entire 2H bull case turns on Thursday.

items.source: Dark Side of the Boom (Goldman Coppersmith note)
items.title: Goldman: AI Rotation From Mag 7 to Semis and Asian Supply Chains — Positioning Fragile
items.idea: Goldman's Coppersmith report: AI remains the core secular trade, but investors moving deeper into Semis and Asian supply chains rather than adding Mag 7 exposure. Positioning becoming fragile: record semiconductor allocations, rising HF leverage, higher Tech volatility. Macro back in control — Fed + front-end rates replaced geopolitics as primary volatility driver. Systematic flows (dealer hedging, leveraged ETF rebalancing) can magnify speed of both rallies and reversals.
items.angle: Rotation from Mag 7 to semis/Asia is the structural AI trade expression for practitioners. But if AI capex bubble concern grows, semis get hit first. Record semiconductor allocation is the crowding signal to watch.
items.tweetA: Goldman's Coppersmith: investors rotating from Mag 7 into Semis and Asian supply chains. Record semiconductor allocations. Rising HF leverage. Problem: when leverage, crowding, dealer gamma, and thin liquidity all get flagged simultaneously, the market starts trading the warning, not the fundamentals.
items.tweetB: Macro is back in control of tech stocks. No longer oil + geopolitics — it's the Fed and front-end rates driving volatility. Goldman says systematic flows don't set direction but can materially increase move speed. Setup for a violent unwind if Fed language hardens. Crowded semis, leveraged HFs, thin liquidity.

items.source: Dark Side of the Boom (Goldman Sachs)
items.title: Goldman: EV Penetration Hits New Global High in May — Oil's Marginal Customer Is Disappearing
items.idea: Goldman's nowcast shows global EV penetration reaching a new high in May 2026, with the increase since February broad-based across the world's largest EV markets — not confined to China. Goldman sees this as a credible new downside channel for global oil demand into 2027. Key insight: higher fuel prices change behavior twice — first in the showroom (accelerating EV adoption), then across the existing vehicle fleet. The real oil risk: the marginal barrel loses its natural customer faster than the market expects.
items.angle: The Hormuz crisis may have permanently accelerated EV adoption. Higher gasoline prices are their own demand cure. The structural thesis for long-dated oil gets weaker every month this dynamic persists.
items.tweetA: Goldman: global EV penetration hit a new high in May. Broad-based — not just China. Fuel prices change behavior twice: first in the showroom (more EV purchases), then across the existing fleet (less driving). The Hormuz crisis may have permanently accelerated the demand shift it was supposed to reverse. Bearish long-dated crude.
items.tweetB: The marginal barrel of oil is losing its natural customer faster than the market expects. Goldman's EV nowcast shows penetration at a new global high in May — and the move is broadening beyond China. The real Hormuz risk isn't the current disruption. It's that the crisis pushed people into EVs and they don't come back.

items.title: Gulf Energy Infrastructure Transformation: Hormuz Shock Accelerates Alternative Routes
items.idea: The Hormuz conflict has sharpened the case for alternative export routes, expanded pipelines, larger storage systems, and more redundancy across the regional energy network. 'Energy security is increasingly becoming a question of optionality.' China's large stockpiles during the crisis functioned as a policy tool — Beijing had time when others had urgency. China may still have meaningful inventory optionality in reserve. The producer that can keep barrels moving when the obvious route is closed holds geopolitical leverage, not just commercial advantage.
items.angle: Gulf energy infrastructure investment is a multi-year structural trade. Saudi ARAMCO alternative pipelines, UAE diversification. China's inventory optionality is an underweighted geopolitical asset — Beijing as the patient player in commodity markets.
items.tweetA: The Hormuz shock taught the Gulf one lesson: dependence on a single maritime chokepoint is a strategic weakness. The next decade is about alternative routes, pipelines, and storage. The producer with options doesn't need to chase price — it lets the market come to it.
items.tweetB: China's stockpiles during the Hormuz crisis were a policy tool, not just inventory. Beijing had time when everyone else had urgency. In commodity markets, that's everything. China may still have meaningful inventory optionality in reserve. Watch how Beijing plays the next Hormuz headline.

items.title: Russia Targets USDC + BNB: 'Unfriendly Crypto' Bill Effective July 1
items.idea: Russia's 'unfriendly crypto' bill takes effect July 1, targeting USDC and BNB with specific commission requirements. Idiosyncratic headwind for BNB that ETH, BTC, and SOL don't face. BNB already lagging the weekend crypto bid and sitting around the $580-590 pivot. VanEck launched VBNB ETF based on BNB's real-world usage, but the Russia regulatory headwind is the near-term negative. A major Wall Street bank filed amended ETH AND SOL spot-ETF applications this weekend.
items.angle: Russia as template for EM crypto restrictions. If the bill creates a playbook that Central Asian or MENA countries follow, USDC's stablecoin dominance faces regulatory erosion in important geographic segments.
items.tweetA: Russia's 'unfriendly crypto' bill hits July 1. Targets USDC and BNB specifically with commission requirements. The one idiosyncratic headwind affecting BNB that ETH, BTC, and SOL don't face. Combined with lagging weekend bid and $580-590 pivot failure. BNB is the one to avoid into July. $BNB
items.tweetB: Russia just created a template: target 'unfriendly' stablecoins with commissions. USDC and BNB are initial targets. If Central Asia, MENA, or Southeast Asia follows — beginning of fragmentation in dollar-pegged stablecoin dominance. Watch which countries copy the playbook in Q3.

items.title: Bitcoin Miners: 20% Unprofitable, 32,000 BTC Sold Q1 — Supply Overhang Caps Rallies
items.idea: ~20% of Bitcoin miners now unprofitable, public miners sold 32,000+ BTC in Q1 — persistent supply overhang under any price bounce. Traders loading bearish bets toward $52,000. BTC range: $73K → $59,100 → recovered $64K → $67,200 → below $62,400 post-FOMC → back to $64K. Polymarket: 67% chance BTC closes June at $64-66K. HIVE Digital pivoting from mining to AI compute via $220M sovereign-AI deal.
items.angle: HIVE's AI pivot is the smart play: mining 20% underwater, but data center infrastructure transfers directly to AI compute. Bifurcation in mining sector — AI-pivoting vs. pure-play miners — is the relative value trade.
items.tweetA: 20% of Bitcoin miners are unprofitable right now. Public miners already sold 32,000+ BTC in Q1. Traders loading shorts toward $52,000. Meanwhile BTC network transactions are near bull-cycle highs. Strong hands absorbing supply from stressed miners. The floor is being built slowly, painfully, correctly. $BTC
items.tweetB: HIVE Digital just pivoted from Bitcoin mining to AI compute with a $220M sovereign-AI deal. Mining is 20% underwater, but data center infrastructure transfers directly to AI. The bifurcation — AI-pivoting vs. pure miners — is the relative value trade. $HIVE

items.title: Post-Record OpEx: Less Gamma, Quarter-End Pension Rebalancing, Fading Buybacks
items.idea: The largest options expiration on record just occurred. Week ahead: less gamma support post-OpEx, possible quarter-end pension rebalancing (equities outperformed bonds → pension funds are structural sellers), fading buybacks, tighter liquidity. Treasury auctions 2/5/7-year notes Tues-Thurs. Bank stress test results from Fed due this week. McClellan Summation Index and breadth metrics sending mixed signals.
items.angle: Quarter-end + post-OpEx + pension rebalancing is a predictable headwind in the final June week. Historically creates 1-2% drawdown opportunities. Bank stress tests could be a positive catalyst if capital return capacity confirmed high.
items.tweetA: The largest options expiration on record just happened. What comes next: less gamma support, quarter-end pension rebalancing (equities outperformed → pension funds are sellers), fading buybacks, tighter liquidity. This week's technical setup is for choppiness. That's not bearish — it's a range-trade setup inside a bull market.
items.tweetB: Bank stress test results this week. If the Fed gives banks a clean bill of health, the next move is buyback authorizations and dividend increases — right when natural buyback activity is fading into quarter-end. The stress test result is a positive catalyst getting underweighted against Iran/PCE noise.

items.source: Princeton Chen / Tiger Capital + Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: Cramer: 'AI Trade Left Hyperscalers in the Dust' — NVDA-Anthropic Partnership Scaling Fast
items.idea: Jim Cramer's call: the AI trade has left hyperscalers (MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) behind, with leadership in pure-play AI names. Nasdaq-100 rebalance adding Astera Labs, CoreWeave, and Nebius reinforces this — pure-play AI infrastructure getting structural index exposure. Jensen Huang: NVDA-Anthropic partnership 'scaling very, very quickly.' SpaceX is now the world's 5th-largest stock, holding 18,712 BTC, with tokenized SPCX trading on Solana. Leadership-rotation debate is the defining narrative for Q3.
items.angle: Hyperscaler lag is either the entry opportunity or the warning sign. If they're lagging because FCF is consumed by capex, Cramer's call is a warning, not a rotation. If pure-play AI leadership continues, second derivative plays (ALAB, NBIS, NVDA) outperform.
items.tweetA: Cramer says 'the AI trade has left the hyperscalers in the dust.' He might be right for the wrong reasons. MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN are lagging not because the AI trade is wrong — but because their FCF is being consumed by capex. The pure-plays have no FCF to consume. Conviction, not confirmation. $NVDA $ALAB
items.tweetB: NVDA-Anthropic partnership 'scaling very, very quickly' per Jensen Huang. Astera Labs, CoreWeave, Nebius join the Nasdaq-100 today. SpaceX is the world's 5th-largest stock. The AI infrastructure trade is going deeper into the supply chain — not rotating back to hyperscalers. This is the second derivative of the AI trade.

items.title: Global Calendar: China LPR Today, Australia CPI Wed, BoJ Summary Wed, Tokyo CPI Fri
items.idea: Key global data this week: China 1-year and 5-year loan prime rates today (Monday); Australia May CPI Wednesday (RBA's next decision point); BoJ Summary of Opinions from its June meeting Wednesday; Australia labor force survey Thursday; Germany Ifo business confidence Wednesday; GfK consumer confidence Thursday; Tokyo CPI for June Friday; ECB consumer expectations survey Friday (inflation expectations in focus, Lagarde speaking). UK politics (Starmer exit) dominates European headlines alongside global flash PMIs.
items.angle: Tokyo CPI Friday is the sleeper catalyst. BoJ under pressure to hike as yen hits 40-year lows. If Tokyo CPI surprises higher, yen strengthens, global carry unwind risk materializes. Asymmetric risk most traders aren't positioned for.
items.tweetA: Tokyo CPI on Friday. BoJ Summary of Opinions on Wednesday. The yen is at 40-year lows. If Tokyo CPI surprises higher, the BoJ is forced to act, the yen strengthens rapidly, carry unwinds. The last time Tokyo CPI surprised it triggered a global liquidity event. This is the macro domino being underweighted against Hormuz noise.
items.tweetB: Australia May CPI Wednesday. Germany Ifo Wednesday. Lagarde speaking Friday. The global data calendar this week is richer than the headline 'PCE Thursday' framing suggests. Each of these could independently move local rates markets. The aggregate is a lot of macro risk to hold into.
